concept: Pending / Awaiting a Needed Action or Condition
description: Awaiting a stated action, decision, item, or condition before its status can change. Pending says only that something remains open and whose move it waits on; work may not have begun, and the expected event may never come. Its neighbor 'noraku' (blocked) is stronger: an obstacle, not a turn in the queue.
sound_symbolism: Air moves freely through light 'ha' before stressed 'so' draws it into a narrower hiss and round vowel. Final 'ru' flicks the tongue and settles into deep 'u'. The contour opens wide, tightens at its center, then turns once before coming to rest.
grammatical_notes: An adjective: 'ha riporu hasoru nai.' (This task is pending.) State what is awaited and who controls or is affected by the wait where relevant. The quality-noun rule supplies pending status. Distinct from 'noraku' (blocked), 'hasi' (wait), 'sholu' (complete), and future marking with 'so'.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Explicit pending status can expose unanswered requests and delayed decisions without blaming the person waiting or pretending the matter is resolved.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Material, weather, drying, curing, growth, and shared availability can all require waiting; naming the state leaves its practical cause open.

tags.activity: work or decision status awaiting a condition
tags.temporal: remaining unresolved until a later event
